text: so regardless of the situation. It had only taken a few days to fail . With the assistance of another bot, Ronin pulled himself smoothly up onto the wall; out of the darkness and into the artificial light. The Clinic loomed ahead, dark from this angle except for the street lamps on the roads surrounding it. The land around it was covered in mostly-green, neatly cropped grass — a lawn. A line of fir trees separated the grounds from the rest of the bot district, though bits of the houses beyond were visible through the boughs . He swung his gaze southwest again, longing for even a glimpse of the ramshackle settlement beyond the wall. If he ran, he could reach her in minutes, could be with his Lara and know she was safe . Instead, he picked his way down through the bent lengths of steel, splintered boards, and cracked chunks of concrete protruding from the wall’s inside face. Perhaps it was meant as much to keep people in as it was to keep them out . Ronin swung his rifle into his hands — it was solid and well-maintained, but its feel was unfamiliar — and joined and the others who’d already crossed the wall. They kneeled along the rear of the building, expanding their perimeter as more men trickled over . Within two minutes, the entirety of Alpha Team was crouched in the grass beside the Clinic . “Place is quiet,” someone remarked . “Shh. Listen .” The wind sighed through the trees and grass, and, more distantly, it howled over the vast, indifferent Dust. Closer, the Clinic’s electric lights hummed. Nearly lost amidst those sounds were two distinct voices, engaged in conversation. Ronin’s audio receptors isolated them, picking the words out from the background noise . “My processors keep going around in circles. Going to wind up with a critical error at some point,” said the first . “I don’t understand why,” replied the other, crackling faintly with static that had nothing to do with the wind. “We’ve seen people come in from the Dust. Logic dictates that there are more settlements out there .” “Logic dictates that even this place shouldn’t exist .” “That logic is flawed. You run diagnostics lately? Your data might be corrupted .” “That’s the whole problem. Logic dictates this place should not exist, yet here it is. If it’s here, there must be other places. But none of those places should exist, either. It’s going to fry my damned CPU if I don’t break the logic chain .” “Why are we talking about this again, anyway? We’ve had the same conversation every time we’re stationed at this post together .” “Because this post is boring enough that I worry my joints will rust up and I’ll be stuck in this spot forever,” the first replied . “Another irrational thought process. We would — ” Ronin cut his audio receptors back to his immediate vicinity. He recognized the first voice; it belonged to Reg, the synth who normally guarded the east road . “At least two gearheads, guarding the main entrance,” Ronin said . “We can take one of these other doors,” someone offered. “There’s at least four that we can see from here .” “No idea if they’re barricaded from within, or just locked,” Ronin responded. “And my mapping only covers the active portion of the building. Maybe fifteen percent of the complex, at best .” “We’re going to have to deal with all of them, at some point,” Maul said . “Would be ideal if we had full control of the Clinic before we attracted attention.” Ronin ran his optics over their surroundings again. Everything was relatively still, relatively quiet. That only meant any sort of alarm would be heard all-too-clearly by the bots deeper in the district . “Yeah. So, we need to make this as quick and quiet as possible. Dodge and Dozer, you’re with me and Ronin. Time to introduce ourselves to these gearheads .” Two synths stepped forward. One was a slight female with short blonde hair. DOZER was painted across the front of her helmet in thick black letters. Ronin’s data contained information on large, powerful, pre-Black Out machines called bulldozers, but it was fragmented . “At some point before the Memory Wipe, someone modified her,” Maul explained. “She’s not retrofitted for combat, like a lot of us were, but her actuators are the most advanced we’ve seen. Might’ve been some experimental shit .” Dozer offered Ronin a lopsided grin and fell into place behind Maul . The Sergeant gestured to the other soldiers. “Gracie, Eisener, Palitto, and Morrison. Flank around the east side. Keep below the windows, and stick to the shadows. I want you in position and ready to open fire if necessary .” The group hurried off, disappearing around an outside corner . “Rest of you, hold tight. Ears on for a signal to advance .” Ronin followed Maul, their footfalls hushed over the soft grass . Were Lara and her team in position yet? Was she safe? For several seconds, the urge to change course threatened to override his current processes . Somehow, he resisted, crossing the lawn to the Clinic’s brick face. He readied his rifle, missing the familiar grooves worn into the grip of his old firearm. His prior weapon had served him reliably for fifty-seven years. The attachment to it was irrational, but he couldn’t deny it . Maul halted at the corner, pressed his back against the wall, and peered around it. Ronin knew the layout well. This section of the building ran north-south. Around the corner, another portion ran perpendicular to this one. The front doors were at their meeting point . Ronin ran his optics over the area. The expanse of short-cropped grass, bordered on one side by a neat row of manicured trees, did not belong in this world — at least not under a leader like Warlord . Though he possessed no
